name: Geb Extended
description: Geb, in Egyptian mythology, is the primordial god of the Earth. He is the son of Shu (air) and Tefnut (moisture), and the husband and brother of Nut (the sky). Geb is often depicted as a man lying beneath Nut, sometimes green-skinned to represent vegetation and fertility. His laughter was believed to cause earthquakes. Geb is a key figure in the Ennead of Heliopolis, a group of nine deities that represent the creation and early history of the world. He played a crucial role in the succession myth, where he inherited the throne from Shu. His role as the Earth god connected him intimately with life, growth, and the physical world. He was also considered a judge of the dead, guiding souls through the underworld. Geb's connection to the earth also made him a symbol of kingship, with pharaohs often associated with him. He is considered a benevolent deity, providing sustenance and stability to the world. He is typically depicted with a goose on his head, symbolizing his connection to the earth. Geb is also associated with minerals and precious stones found within the earth, further emphasizing his dominion over the terrestrial realm. His separation from Nut by Shu marked the creation of space for life to flourish.
subtitle: God of the Earth and Fertility

keyMyths.title: Separation of Geb and Nut
keyMyths.description: Shu separates Geb and Nut to create space between the Earth and sky, allowing for the creation of the world. This act is crucial for the development of life on Earth.

keyMyths.title: Geb's Inheritance
keyMyths.description: Geb inherits the throne from Shu, solidifying his role as the ruler of the Earth. This marks a significant stage in the succession of gods within the Ennead.

keyMyths.title: Geb as Judge of the Dead
keyMyths.description: Geb is involved in judging the deceased in the underworld, guiding them through the afterlife. His role highlights his connection to both the living world and the realm of the dead.

sources.author: E.A. Wallis Budge
sources.description: Comprehensive overview of Egyptian deities and their roles.
